title: notholaena sulphurea var. californica
intro: Notholaena sulphurea var. californica, also known as California golden fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Pteridaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it beneficial for treating various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and acting as a natural antiseptic to prevent infection. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Notholaena sulphurea var. californica can be made into a tea or infused in oil to create a topical treatment for skin and wound care.
